segments.p2-2-g1: Но is the coordinating conjunction 'but/however' introducing a contrast. Кончалась is the imperfective past (third person singular, feminine) of кончаться 'to end' and agrees with полоса (feminine); полоса here means a period or streak and is in the nominative as the subject. Моего развратика is a genitive attribute 'of my libertine (period)', where развратика (genitive of развратник) names the type/person associated with the streak.
segments.p2-2-g2: И 'and' links the second clause. Мне is the dative 'to me', introducing an experiencer in an impersonal construction. Становилось is the neuter impersonal past of становиться 'to become' and combines with the predicate тошно to mean 'I began to feel nauseous'. Ужасно is an adverbial intensifier 'terribly' modifying тошно 'nauseous/sick'.
segments.p2-2-g3: Наступало is the neuter impersonal past of наступать 'to begin/occur', here meaning 'set in' or 'came on'. Раскаяние is a nominative noun 'repentance/remorse' functioning as the subject of наступало; the whole clause is an impersonal way to say 'repentance began'.
segments.p2-2-g4: Я is the nominative subject 'I'. Его is an accusative/object form 'it/him' referring back to раскаяние ('it'), and гнал is the past tense (masculine singular) of гнать 'to drive/chase', here meaning 'I drove/chased it away'. The colon sets up an explanatory or emphatic afterthought.
segments.p2-2-g5: Слишком is an adverb 'too/excessively' and уж is an emphatic particle strengthening слишком (here 'quite/very much'). Тошнило is the neuter impersonal past of тошнить 'to feel nauseous', used impersonally to say 'it was nauseating' or 'I felt sick'.
segments.p2-2-g6: Мало-помалу is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'little by little/gradually'. Я is the subject 'I'. The comma marks a slight pause before the parenthetical particle that follows.
segments.p2-2-g7: Однако же is a two-word combination where однако 'however/nevertheless' is a conjunctive adverb and же is an emphatic particle reinforcing the contrast; together they mean 'however/though'.
segments.p2-2-g8: И is 'and' or 'also' linking to the previous idea. К is the preposition 'to/toward' (here in the variant ко before certain pronouns but used as 'to'), этому is the dative demonstrative 'to this/it' referring to the unpleasant feeling or situation, and привыкал is the imperfective past of привыкать 'to get used to', indicating a gradual habituation.
segments.p2-2-g9: Я is the subject 'I'. Ко is the allomorph of к used before certain pronouns, meaning 'to/toward'; всему is the dative 'to everything' (i.e., 'everything' as the object of getting used to). Привыкал is the imperfective past of привыкать 'was getting used to/used to', describing a habitual or ongoing past process.
segments.p2-2-g10: То есть is the fixed explanatory phrase 'that is' or 'that is to say' (то + есть 'is'). Не то что is a concessive idiom meaning 'not that (I) [simply] got used to it'—то что here introduces the clause being negated. Привыкал repeats the verb 'got used to', showing the contrast between mere habituation and what follows.
segments.p2-2-g11: А is a contrastive conjunction 'but/whereas'. Как-то is an adverb meaning 'somehow' or 'in some way' (как with the particle -то); добровольно is an adverb 'voluntarily/willingly'. Соглашался is the imperfective past of соглашаться 'to agree/consent', and переносить is the infinitive 'to endure/tolerate'—together 'consented to endure'.
segments.p2-2-g12: "Но" is the adversative conjunction 'but' introducing a contrast. "У меня был выход" uses the construction 'у + genitive' to express possession ('I had'); "был" is the past tense of быть (masculine singular) completing the verbal predicate, and "выход" ('way out/escape') is the noun possessed.
segments.p2-2-g13: The phrase "все примирявший" is a post-nominal past active participle (примирявший) that modifies "выход"; "все" here is the object of the participle meaning 'everything' (i.e. 'reconciling everything'), so the whole phrase reads 'an escape that reconciled everything'.
segments.p2-2-g14: "это -" is a resumptive demonstrative ('this was') with a dash for emphasis and apposition. "Спасаться" is the reflexive infinitive meaning 'to take refuge/seek refuge' or 'to save oneself'; it names the content of that 'escape'. "Во" is the preposition 'in', and the phrase "все прекрасное и высокое" uses neuter substantivized adjectives ('all that is beautiful and lofty') — "все" = 'all', "прекрасное" and "высокое" are abstract neuter nouns formed from adjectives.
segments.p2-2-g15: "Конечно" is a parenthetical adverb ('of course') qualifying the previous clause; it is an intensifying/discourse marker and does not change case or agreement.
segments.p2-2-g16: "В мечтах" is a prepositional phrase: "в" + locative plural of "мечта" and means 'in dreams' (i.e. in one's daydreams or fantasies). It specifies the manner/place in which he took refuge.
segments.p2-2-g17: Subject-verb inversion for emphasis: the past tense verb "мечтал" (masculine singular) precedes the pronoun "я" ('I'). "Ужасно" is an adverb intensifier here meaning 'very/terribly' and modifies the verb 'dreamed'.
segments.p2-2-g18: "Мечтал по три месяца сряду" uses the distributive/temporal "по + accusative" (по три месяца) to mean 'for three months at a time' or 'three months in a row'; "сряду" reinforces 'in succession'.
segments.p2-2-g19: "Забившись" is an adverbial participle (деепричастие) from "забиться" meaning 'having hidden/crept into' and expresses the attendant circumstance; it combines with "в свой угол" ('into my corner') where "свой" agrees with the subject 'I'.
segments.p2-2-g20: "И уж поверьте" is a parenthetical appeal: "поверьте" is the polite/plural imperative 'believe (me)', and "уж" is an intensifying particle here ('indeed'/'do believe me').
segments.p2-2-g21: Subordinate clause introduced by "что" ('that'): "в эти мгновения" ('in those moments') sets the time, and the predicate "я не похож был на того господина" uses the adjective "похож" with "не" for negation plus the past auxiliary "был"; the comparison uses the preposition "на" with the accusative "того господина" ('that gentleman').
segments.p2-2-g22: "Который" is a relative pronoun ('who/which') agreeing in gender/number with "того господина" and introduces the relative clause that follows.
segments.p2-2-g23: "В смятении куриного сердца" is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in the agitation/confusion of a chicken's heart'; "смятении" is the noun in prepositional, and "куриного сердца" is a genitive attribute 'of a chicken's heart' ("сердца" here refers to feelings/emotional heart).
segments.p2-2-g24: "Пришивал" is the imperfective past tense 'was sewing on' (implying the repeated or ongoing action) with the direct object "немецкий бобрик" (accusative inanimate, form identical to nominative) and the dative/locative goal "к воротнику своей шинели" ('onto the collar of his greatcoat'); "своей" is a reflexive possessive referring back to the subject.
segments.p2-2-g25: Я (subject) + делался is the past tense of the reflexive/mediopassive verb делаться (imperfective), meaning “to become” or “to get to be.” The noun героем is in the instrumental case, which is the standard case used after становиться/делаться to indicate the role or state someone changes into. Вдруг is an adverb placed after the verb for emphasis: literally “became suddenly a hero.”
segments.p2-2-g26: The phrase begins with the direct object 'Моего десятивершкового поручика' placed before the subject for emphasis; because the noun is animate masculine, its accusative form coincides with the genitive ('моего ... поручика'). 'Десятивершкового' is a compound adjectival form (from 'вершок', an old small unit of length) describing his stature. The core clause 'я бы ... не пустил' uses the conditional particle бы with the past-tense verb 'пустил' to mean 'would not have let'; 'даже и' is an emphatic combination meaning 'even', 'с визитом' (с + instrumental) means 'on a visit', 'к себе' ‘to my place’, and 'тогда' locates the action in time — the negation 'не' directly negates the verb.
segments.p2-2-g27: Я (subject) + даже и (combined particles for emphasis, 'even') precede the infinitive представить which here (with себя and the accusative его) forms the phrase представить себе кого — 'to picture/imag​ine someone to oneself'. The modal past не мог negates ability ('could not'), and тогда is a temporal adverb placed at the end ('then').
segments.p2-2-g28: Что такое ... были мои мечты is an inverted/exploratory phrasing meaning 'what my dreams were like'; были is past plural to agree with мечты. The second clause как мог я ими довольствоваться uses мог + infinitive to ask 'how could I content myself with them' (ими is instrumental required by довольствоваться). The dash leads into the comment об этом трудно сказать теперь, where об этом ('about this') is the topic and трудно сказать is an impersonal predicate 'it's hard to say', with теперь 'now' at the end.
segments.p2-2-g29: но тогда я этим довольствовался is a simple past statement: но 'but', тогда 'at that time', я subject, этим is instrumental pronoun referring back to the dreams or their content, and довольствовался is the past reflexive verb 'was content with'. The verb governs the instrumental case (этим).
segments.p2-2-g30: Впрочем is a discourse particle meaning 'however' or 'moreover' that introduces a qualification; it stands alone and signals the following contrast or clarification.
segments.p2-2-g31: я (subject) + ведь (particle 'after all') and и (here adding the sense 'even') combine to stress the statement; теперь 'now' locates it in the present; этим is the instrumental pronoun ('with it/them') required by довольствуюсь, which is present tense first-person singular 'I am content with', and отчасти 'partly' modifies the degree.
segments.p2-2-g32: Мечты is the plural subject. Особенно modifies the comparative adjectives слаще и сильнее ('sweeter and stronger/more intense'), which are used metaphorically to qualify how the dreams came; приходили is past plural 'came' (or 'occurred to me') and ко мне is the dative 'to me'. После развратика is a temporal/occasional phrase 'after the libertine/debauchee' (lit. 'after the libertine').
segments.p2-2-g33: This repeats приходили and then uses с + instrumental nouns (раскаянием и слезами) to indicate manner or accompaniment: 'they came with repentance and with tears.' The parallel instrumental phrases describe the way the dreams manifested.
segments.p2-2-g34: Another coordinated instrumental phrase с проклятиями и восторгами continues the list of accompaniments: 'with curses and (with) raptures/enthusiasms.' The nouns are in instrumental plural governed by с and linked by и.
segments.p2-2-g135: The main clause uses the present plural verb плачут and целуют to describe people’s ongoing actions toward the narrator; меня is the accusative object of целуют. The parenthetical phrase (иначе что же бы они были за болваны) is a rhetorical aside introduced by иначе (“otherwise”), containing the conditional particle бы and the emphatic particle же — что … были за is a colloquial rhetorical construction meaning “what would they be (but) …,” with за here functioning to classify (roughly ‘as’/‘but’). Word order is typical for Russian rhetorical questions: particle sequence иначе + что + же + бы + subject + verb + nominal predicate.
segments.p2-2-g136: а marks a contrastive turn to the narrator; the verbs иду and разбиваю are in the present tense, giving a vivid or habitual sense ("I go… and I smash"). The infinitive проповедовать follows иду to indicate purpose/intention ("I go to preach"). Adjectives босой и голодный agree with the subject я and appear after the verb for narrative emphasis. Под + instrumental (Аустерлицем) indicates location or the famous battlefield ("at/near Austerlitz").
segments.p2-2-g137: Затем is a temporal adverb meaning “then/afterwards.” Играется is a reflexive/passive-form verb used impersonally here to mean “is played” (of music), with марш as the theme/subject. The short clause sets one item in a sequence of events.
segments.p2-2-g138: Выдается is another passive-form verb meaning “is issued/granted,” taking амнистия as its patient; the structure is the same impersonal/passive reporting of events in sequence.
segments.p2-2-g139: Папа is the subject (here, the Pope), соглашается is a reflexive verb meaning “agrees,” and it governs the infinitive выехать (“to leave”), so the clause means “the Pope agrees to leave.” Из + genitive (из Рима) marks origin, while в + accusative (в Бразилию) marks direction/destination.
segments.p2-2-g140: Затем begins another item in the list. Бал is the noun “ball/dance,” followed by the prepositional phrase для всей Италии (для + genitive indicates purpose/benefit “for all Italy”) and the locative на вилле Боргезе (на + prepositional indicates location at the Villa Borghese).
segments.p2-2-g141: Что here is a relative pronoun linking back to "вилле Боргезе," introducing a subclause meaning “which is on the shore of Lake Como.” На берегу uses preposition + locative (берегу is prepositional/locative) to indicate position; озера Комо is a genitive phrase naming the lake.
segments.p2-2-g142: Так как is the causal conjunction “since/because.” Переносится is present passive/reflexive "is moved/relocated," and нарочно (= deliberately) modifies it. Для этого случая uses для + genitive (purpose/occasion), and в Рим (в + accusative) indicates the destination to which the lake is humorously said to be moved.
segments.p2-2-g143: Затем continues the sequence. Сцена в кустах is a short noun phrase meaning “a scene in the bushes” (сцена as a public scandal or an actual stage scene), and и т. is the abbreviation for и тому подобное (“etc.”) closing the list of tableaux.
segments.p2-2-g144: This is the single-letter abbreviation 'д.' (dot included) used to stand for the final element of the common Russian abbreviation 'и т. д.' (и так далее / и тому подобное). Grammatically it functions as an abbreviated noun/adverbial element meaning 'and so on' or 'etc.'; the period shows it is an abbreviation.
segments.p2-2-g145: A comma punctuation mark separating the preceding text from the following abbreviated phrase; it indicates continuation or addition and has no lexical meaning itself.
segments.p2-2-g146: Contains the conjunction 'и' plus the abbreviation 'т.' (part of 'и т. д.'). Here 'и' is the coordinating 'and' used within the conventional abbreviation that means 'and so on' or 'etc.' The 'т.' is an abbreviated element (from 'так' / 'тому' in different full forms) and together the fragment functions as an elliptical form of 'и т. д.'
segments.p2-2-g57: Либо begins a correlative pair (либо ... либо) that sets up alternatives; герой is a nominative noun serving as the first alternative (no explicit verb in this verbless clause). Together they mean “either (a) hero.”
segments.p2-2-g58: The second member of the alternative pair: либо again marks the alternative, and грязь (nominative) names the opposite pole — here an insulting/pejorative ‘filth’ opposed to ‘hero.’
segments.p2-2-g59: средины is the genitive of средина (‘middle’), used with the impersonal past negation не было to express absence (literally, ‘middle there was not’ → ‘there was no middle ground’). не было is the fixed past-tense existential negation.
segments.p2-2-g60: Это-то is the demonstrative with the emphatic particle -то (’this very thing/precisely that’); меня is the accusative object; и here is a colloquial particle adding emphasis (‘indeed/also’); сгубило is the perfective past (neuter form, agreeing with the neuter demonstrative) of сгубить ‘to ruin/destroy’—so the phrase means ‘this very thing ruined me.’
segments.p2-2-g61: потому что is the causal conjunction ‘because’; в грязи is a prepositional phrase ‘in the filth’ (грязи in the prepositional case); я утешал себя тем is ‘I comforted/reassured myself by/with that,’ where тем is a demonstrative/instrumental placeholder that will be explained by the following clause introduced by что.
segments.p2-2-g62: The subordinate clause introduced by что: в другое время is a temporal phrase ‘at another time / on other occasions’; бываю is the habitual/present sense of бывать (‘to be/sometimes be’), used with я to mean ‘I am (at times)’; герой is the predicate nominative with the copula implied.
segments.p2-2-g63: а introduces contrast (‘whereas/and’); герой is the grammatical subject; прикрывал is the imperfective past of прикрывать here meaning ‘would cover / concealed’ (habitual or descriptive past); собой is the instrumental pronoun meaning ‘by/with himself’ (i.e. by virtue of being a hero); грязь is the object ‘the filth’ — ‘and the hero covered up the filth.’
segments.p2-2-g64: Обыкновенному is an adjective in the dative singular (‘to/for the ordinary [one]’) — here it begins the phrase that will name the experiencer (‘to the ordinary man’). The noun follows in the next segment.
segments.p2-2-g65: Дескать is a parenthetical colloquial particle meaning ‘they say’ / ‘so they claim,’ used to introduce a reported or proverbial remark (here: ‘to the ordinary man, they say…’).
segments.p2-2-g66: человеку is the dative noun that completes the phrase ‘to the ordinary man’; стыдно is an impersonal predicate meaning ‘it is shameful/embarrassing’; грязниться is the reflexive infinitive ‘to soil oneself / to get dirty.’ Together: ‘to the ordinary man, they say, it is shameful to get dirty.’
segments.p2-2-g67: а introduces contrast again; герой is subject; слишком высок uses the adverb слишком (‘too/excessively’) with the short-form adjective высок (predicative short form of высокий, here meaning ‘lofty/elevated’) — ‘whereas the hero is too lofty.’
segments.p2-2-g68: чтоб introduces a subordinate clause of result/purpose (here negative result: ‘so as to / that he should’); совсем is an adverb ‘completely’; загрязниться is the perfective reflexive verb ‘to become soiled/ sullied’ — the clause means ‘to become completely soiled.’
segments.p2-2-g69: Следственно is a linking adverb meaning ‘consequently/therefore,’ signaling a logical result drawn from the preceding claim (hero too lofty to be wholly sullied → therefore ...).
segments.p2-2-g70: можно with an infinitive is an impersonal modal construction meaning ‘it is possible/allowed to…’; грязниться is the infinitive ‘to get dirty.’ So the clause reads ‘so, consequently, one may get dirty’ (i.e. it’s permissible).
segments.p2-2-g71: "Замечательно" is an evaluative sentence adverb (adjective used predicatively in neuter form) meaning 'it is remarkable' or 'remarkably' and functions as an introductory comment on the whole clause that follows.
segments.p2-2-g72: The clause begins with the subordinating particle "что" ('that') introducing reported content. "эти приливы" is the nominative plural subject ('these surges'); the quoted phrase in genitive "всего прекрасного и высокого" specifies the content of the surges ('of all that is beautiful and lofty'). "приходили ко мне" uses the imperfective past "приходили" to mean 'would come/occur to me' (habitual or repeated occurrence), with the dative "ко мне" marking the experiencer. "и во время развратика" is a temporal phrase ('even during the time of my depravity'), where "во время" governs the noun in the genitive.
segments.p2-2-g73: "и именно тогда" is an emphatic temporal adverbial sequence: "и" ('and/even'), "именно" ('precisely/exactly' — emphasis), "тогда" ('then'), together meaning 'and precisely then'.
segments.p2-2-g74: "когда" introduces a subordinate temporal clause ('when'). "я уже на самом дне находился" literally 'I already was located on the very bottom' — "находился" is the past imperfective reflexive verb 'to be/found oneself in a state' (masculine singular), and "на самом дне" is a fixed prepositional phrase ('at the very bottom') with "самом" adding the 'very' emphasis.
segments.p2-2-g75: "приходили так" — "приходили" repeats the imperfective past 'would come/occur', and "так" qualifies manner ('in such a way' / 'thus'), preparing the following adverbial description.
segments.p2-2-g76: "отдельными вспышечками" is an instrumental plural phrase used adverbially to describe manner: both words agree in instrumental case and mean 'as separate little flashes' (instrumental often expresses the means or manner in which something happens).
segments.p2-2-g77: "как будто" ('as if') introduces a comparison; "напоминая" is a present-gerund (деепричастие) derived from "напоминать" and modifies the main verb "приходили" — 'coming as if reminding (me) of themselves'. "о себе" ('about themselves' / 'of themselves') is the object of the gerund and points back to the surges.
segments.p2-2-g78: "но не истребляли" is a negative statement: "но" ('but'), "не" negates the verb, and "истребляли" is past imperfective plural of "истреблять" meaning 'destroy/annihilate' — here 'they did not destroy/eradicate (it)'.
segments.p2-2-g79: "однако ж" is a twice-emphatic connective: "однако" ('however/on the other hand') strengthened by the particle "ж" (literary/colloquial emphatic particle), reinforcing the contrast with the previous clause.
segments.p2-2-g80: "развратика своим появлением" is the object and instrumental means: "развратика" is the (accusative) object being affected ('the debauchery' / 'my debauchery' personified), and "своим появлением" is an instrumental noun phrase ('by their appearance') expressing the means by which destruction might have occurred — but did not.
segments.p2-2-g81: "напротив" here is a discourse adverb meaning 'on the contrary', signaling that what follows opposes the expectation set up earlier.
segments.p2-2-g82: "как будто подживляли его контрастом и приходили ровно на столько" — "как будто" ('as if'); "подживляли" is past imperfective plural of "подживлять" meaning 'kept alive / sustained a little' (literary/colloquial), with "его" ('it/him') referring back to the debauchery; "контрастом" is instrumental ('by/with contrast'), expressing the means; the conjunction "и" links to a second verb "приходили" ('they came/occurred'), and "ровно на столько" is a measure phrase 'exactly by/as much' specifying the precise quantity or degree.
segments.p2-2-g83: "сколько было нужно для хорошего соуса" is an impersonal measure clause: "сколько" ('as much as/whatever amount'), "было нужно" is the impersonal past ("было" + predicate "нужно") meaning 'was needed', and "для хорошего соуса" ('for a good sauce') is a genitive phrase expressing purpose or use; together 'as much as was needed for a good sauce.'
segments.p2-2-g84: Соус is the grammatical subject (nominative) meaning 'sauce'; тут is a colloquial adverb 'here/at this point' placed after the subject for emphasis. состоял is the past tense, masculine, singular of состоять 'to consist (of)' and governs the preposition из with the following genitive objects: из противоречия и страдания ('of contradiction and suffering').
segments.p2-2-g85: This is a continuation of the list introduced by из: из мучительного внутреннего анализа is a genitive noun phrase. мучительного and внутреннего are adjectives in the genitive singular, modifying анализ (also genitive singular); together they mean 'of an agonizing inner analysis.'
segments.p2-2-g86: The clause begins with и linking back to the previous clause. все эти мученья и мученьица is a noun phrase (all these torments and diminutive torments) where все modifies эти; both nouns are the plural accusative or nominative depending on clause alignment, but here they are subjects of придавали. придавали is the imperfective past plural of придавать 'to impart' (they used to impart). какую-то is the indefinite phrase ('some sort of') formed by какая + -то; пикантность is the direct object 'piquancy'.
segments.p2-2-g87: даже is an adverb 'even' adding emphasis. смысл is a second object 'a meaning' that придавали also imparted, and моему развратику is a dative phrase 'to my debauchery' (моему = dative singular of мой; развратику is dative singular), indicating the recipient/target of that meaning.
segments.p2-2-g88: одним словом is an idiom meaning 'in a word' or 'in short' (instrumental singular of слово used adverbially) serving as a resumptive connector before the concluding clause.
segments.p2-2-g89: исполняли is past plural of исполнять 'to perform/fulfil' agreeing with the plural noun phrase from earlier; вполне is an adverb 'completely/quite'; должность here means 'the role/office' (accusative) and хорошего соуса is a genitive attributive phrase 'of a good sauce' (хорошего in genitive singular modifying соуса) — together: 'they quite fulfilled the role of a good sauce.'
segments.p2-2-g90: Все это = a noun phrase 'all this/that' (Все modifies это); даже is an adverbial particle meaning 'even' adding emphasis. Было is the past tense, neuter form of быть used here as the copula ('was'). Не без is an idiomatic negative construction meaning 'not without' (so it asserts that something positive exists), and некоторой глубины is a genitive phrase governed by без: 'of some / a certain depth' (некоторой is genitive feminine singular to agree with глубины).
segments.p2-2-g91: Да и is a discourse particle here ('and / and besides') introducing a rhetorical question. Мог ли бы я is the modal verb мочь in past tense with the enclitic ли (marks the question) and the particle бы (conditional/potential), giving 'could I have' or 'would I have been able to'. Согласиться на uses the reflexive infinitive 'to agree/consent to' with preposition на + accusative; простой is the first adjective in a series modifying the noun phrase that follows.
segments.p2-2-g92: Пошлый is another adjective in the enumerative series; here it means 'vulgar' or 'banal' and continues to qualify the same (implied) noun.
segments.p2-2-g93: Непосредственный is the next adjective in the list; literally 'direct' or 'straightforward', it likewise modifies the implied noun and keeps the paratactic string of qualities.
segments.p2-2-g94: Писарский развратишко is the noun phrase: писарский ('scribal' or 'bureaucratic') + развратишко (a diminutive/derogatory noun 'lecher/degenerate'); the speaker is belittling the role. И links the infinitives: (согласиться на ... и вынести) — вынести here is the perfective infinitive 'to bear/endure' (one of its senses), and на себе ('on oneself') emphasizes that the speaker would bear it personally. Всю эту грязь is the accusative object 'all this filth' (всю agrees with грязь).
segments.p2-2-g95: Что ж is an interrogative 'what' with the particle ж adding emphasis/colloquial force. Бы могло is the conditional/potential construction (бы + могло, neuter past of мочь) used impersonally with infinitives to mean 'could have' or 'might have'. Тогда 'then/in that case' sets the time/frame. В ней is a prepositional phrase 'in it' (ней after preposition в), and прельстить (perfective infinitive) takes меня as its object: 'to entice/beguile me'. И coordinates the second infinitive выманить ('to lure out'), followed by the adverbial ночью ('at night') and the directional phrase на улицу ('onto the street'). The two infinitives share the modal могло.
segments.p2-2-g96: Нет-с is an interjection; Нет = 'no' and the attached -с is a colloquial/emphatic particle making the negation more pointed (here functioning like 'no —' or 'no indeed').
segments.p2-2-g97: У меня была is the standard possessive construction with у + genitive pronoun + past tense of быть, meaning 'I had'. Была agrees in gender and number with лазейка (feminine singular). Благородная лазейка = 'a noble loophole' (благородная modifies лазейка; the phrase is ironical). На все is a prepositional phrase meaning 'for everything' (на + accusative).
segments.p2-2-g147: The single letter "д." is the final letter of the standard abbreviation "и т. д." (и так далее), meaning "and so on/et cetera." Here it stands alone as the clipped end of that abbreviation; the period marks that abbreviation.
segments.p2-2-g148: This is an elliptical, colloquial question. "Будто" functions as a parenthetical particle meaning "as if," and "не знаете" is 2nd person plural (or polite singular) present indicative of "знать" ("you do not know"); the dash and word order mark a sarcastic or rhetorical tone.
segments.p2-2-g149: "Вы" is the polite or plural 2nd-person pronoun "you." "Скажете" is the 2nd-person plural future (or a rhetorical future) of "сказать," meaning "you will say" or "you'll say," used here to report the listener's anticipated objection.
segments.p2-2-g150: The clause begins with the subordinating conjunction "что" ("that"). "Пошло" here is not the verb "пойти" but the short neuter form of the adjective "пошлый" meaning "vulgar/tawdry" (this sense is not among the supplied verb senses). "И" links the two qualities, and "подло" is an adverbial/short-form adjective meaning "base" or "mean." The infinitive phrase "выводить ... на рынок" ("to bring out/put on the market") follows and is being characterized as "пошло и подло."
segments.p2-2-g151: "После" is the preposition "after" and takes the genitive. "Стольких" is the genitive plural of "столький," meaning "so many/so much," modifying the genitive plural nouns "упоений и слез" ("raptures/intoxicating experiences and tears"). The whole phrase gives the temporal circumstance "after so many raptures and tears."
segments.p2-2-g152: "В которых" is a relative pronoun phrase (preposition + genitive plural) referring back to "упоений и слез" = "in which." "Я" is the subject "I," "сам" is an emphatic reflexive meaning "myself," and "признался" is the past tense (perfective) of "признаться," "I confessed" or "I admitted (to them)." The clause means "which I myself confessed to/owned up to."
segments.p2-2-g153: "Отчего" is an interrogative adverb "why/for what reason." The particle "же" adds emphasis or impatience. "Подло" is the predicate adjective/adverb "base/mean." The final "-с" is not the preposition "с" here but a colloquial enclitic particle attached for ironic or dialectal emphasis (an older/colloquial question-tagging particle); the whole is a sharp rhetorical question: "Why should that be base?"
segments.p2-2-g35: Бывали is the past plural/imperfective of бывать used existentially: 'there were' or 'there used to be'. мгновения is nominative plural 'moments'. такого положительного упоения is a genitive singular noun phrase ('of such positive rapture') that qualifies мгновения; word order places the existential verb first and the genitive phrase after the noun.
segments.p2-2-g36: такого счастья is another genitive noun phrase parallel to the previous one ('of such happiness'), repeating and amplifying the quality of those moments; the genitive links it to мгновения as descriptive measure/quality.
segments.p2-2-g37: что introduces a result clause: 'that ...'. даже is an adverb 'even' adding emphasis. малейшей насмешки is genitive singular 'the slightest mockery' (malейшей is a diminutive/superlative form). внутри меня 'within me/inside me' is a prepositional/locative phrase. не ощущалось is an impersonal/passive past construction ('was not felt'); the whole clause means 'that not even the slightest mockery was felt inside me.'
segments.p2-2-g38: ей-богу is an interjective oath meaning 'I swear to God' (colloquial). Grammatically it preserves dative forms ('to her / to God') but functions here as a fixed exclamation, not a literal dative phrase.
segments.p2-2-g39: Была is past tense (feminine singular) of быть used existentially: 'there was.' вера is nominative singular and is the noun governed by the verb; the verb is stated once and applies to the following nouns as well.
segments.p2-2-g40: надежда is a simple nominative noun in a short list ('hope'); the verb 'Была' from the previous segment is understood to apply here too.
segments.p2-2-g41: любовь is the final item in the nominative list ('love'), completing the triad introduced by the single past verb 'Была.'
segments.p2-2-g42: То-то и есть is an emphatic idiom meaning 'that is precisely it' or 'that's exactly the point'; the duplicated то is an intensifying repetition and есть is the copula 'is.'
segments.p2-2-g43: что introduces the subordinate clause 'that ...'. я слепо верил тогда: я (I) is subject, слепо ('blindly') is an adverb modifying верил (past imperfective 'believed'—ongoing/habitual belief in the past), and тогда ('then') situates the time.
segments.p2-2-g44: что каким-то чудом is a short clause meaning 'that by some miracle.' каким-то is an indefinite instrumental phrase ('by some sort/kind of'), with чудом in the instrumental case denoting the means ('by a miracle').
segments.p2-2-g45: каким-нибудь внешним обстоятельством is another instrumental phrase ('by some external circumstance') where каким-нибудь is the indefinite modifier. все это is the subject 'all this / all of it', and вдруг раздвинется is a future, 3rd-person reflexive/intransitive verb 'will suddenly move apart / be pushed aside'—together 'all this would suddenly be displaced/opened up.'
segments.p2-2-g46: расширится is a synonymous future verb ('will expand / broaden'), reinforcing the previous verb and describing how 'all this' would change.
segments.p2-2-g47: вдруг представится is 'suddenly will present itself' (представится is reflexive/intransitive 'present itself'), горизонт is the nominative subject 'a horizon', and соответственной деятельности ('of corresponding/appropriate activity') is a genitive noun phrase qualifying horizon.
segments.p2-2-g48: благотворной is an adjective (here in the same agreement as соответственной) describing the activity as 'beneficial' or 'salutary'; it's part of a list of qualities that the forthcoming activity will have.
segments.p2-2-g49: прекрасной и continues the adjectival list ('beautiful / splendid, and ...'), with и simply coordinating the next item.
segments.p2-2-g50: главное is a parenthetical neuter nominal meaning 'the main thing' or 'above all,' introducing what the narrator regards as the most important quality to follow.
segments.p2-2-g51: совсем готовой states that the activity would be 'completely ready' (совсем = completely, готовой = ready, agreeing in case/gender with деятельность in the earlier genitive phrase). The parenthetical '(какой именно - я никогда не знал,' is the narrator admitting 'what exactly (kind) — I never knew,' where я никогда не знал is a straightforward past negative ('I never knew').
segments.p2-2-g52: но главное is a short concessive transition 'but the main thing ...' (но = but), resuming the narrator's emphasis after the parenthesis.
segments.p2-2-g53: - совсем готовой) closes the parenthesis and repeats the important point: 'entirely ready' — here the dash and repetition emphasize that readiness above precise form.
segments.p2-2-g54: и вот я выступлю вдруг на свет божий: и (and) + вот (a particle meaning 'then/so/now' drawing attention) + я выступлю (1st person future 'I will step forward/appear') + вдруг (suddenly). на свет божий is an idiom 'into the light of day / into public view' (свет here = 'the world/public sphere', божий = 'God's').
segments.p2-2-g55: чуть ли не ... на белом коне и не в лавровом венке is a set phrase meaning 'almost as if on a white horse and even in a laurel wreath.' чуть ли не = 'almost / nearly'; ли is the enclitic particle used in the idiom; на белом коне = 'on a white horse'; и не в лавровом венке uses the second не as colloquial emphatic 'even' in this context (not a literal negation) and в лавровом венке = 'in a laurel wreath.'
segments.p2-2-g56: Второстепенной роли is in the genitive (literally 'of a secondary role') functioning as the object/concept the speaker could not comprehend; the genitive is common after negation and with the verb 'понять' in this colloquial construction. я и понять не мог uses и as a particle meaning 'even' ('I couldn't even understand'), понять (infinitive) + не мог (past of мочь with negation) expresses inability. и вот именно потому-то links the clauses ('and that's precisely why') — вот именно = 'precisely', потому-то = 'for that very reason'. в действительности 'in reality' qualifies the assertion; очень спокойно modifies занимал (past 'occupied' / 'took') and последнюю is the feminine accusative 'the last one' (росль implied) referring back to роль.
segments.p2-2-g154: Неужели is an interrogative particle used to form a (often rhetorical) surprised question — here 'do you really...'. It precedes the subject вы and the finite verb думаете (2nd person plural present indicative) in normal S-V order; the comma signals the start of the subordinate clause introduced by что that follows.
segments.p2-2-g155: The initial что introduces a content subordinate clause ('that ...'). я стыжусь is the 1st person singular present of стыдиться, taking a genitive object (всего этого = 'of all this'). The coordinating и что begins a second subordinate clause ('and that ...'). In все это было глупее the neuter past было agrees with the neuter pronoun всё/это; глупее is the comparative ('more foolish'). The phrase хотя чего бы то ни было is an idiomatic comparative intensifier meaning 'than anything at all' — здесь хотя + чего + бы + то ни было creates 'than anything whatsoever'. Finally в вашей is the preposition + possessive, the head noun (жизни) is postponed and appears in the next segment (the address господа is interpolated).
segments.p2-2-g156: Господа is a vocative/direct-address form ('gentlemen'). It interrupts the main prepositional phrase (в вашей жизни) for rhetorical effect; the comma shows that this is a direct address, not part of the subordinate clause's core grammar.
segments.p2-2-g157: Жизни is the noun completing the prepositional phrase в вашей жизни ('in your life(s)'). With в it is in the prepositional case (the form here coincides with genitive), and the question mark closes the whole interrogative sentence begun by Неужели.
segments.p2-2-g158: И к тому же is a set connective meaning 'and besides' or 'moreover' (к + тому + же with же emphatic). Поверьте is the polite/imperative 2nd person plural of поверить ('believe'), used as an appeal to the audience; the comma introduces the subordinate clause starting with что.
segments.p2-2-g159: Что introduces the subordinate statement. У меня expresses possession ('I had') using у + genitive (меня). Кой-что is an indefinite compound meaning 'something' (кой + что). Было составлено is a neuter past passive construction: было + short/passive past participle составлено — the neuter agrees with the indefinite кой-что. Вовсе недурно is an adverbial qualifier meaning 'quite/not at all bad' → 'rather well.'
segments.p2-2-g162: Не всё же combines negation Не with всё ('everything') plus the particle же for emphasis/contrast — 'not everything, after all'. Происходило is the neuter past of происходить ('happened/took place') agreeing with neuter всё. На озере Комо is a prepositional phrase indicating location (на + озере) naming Lake Como.
segments.p2-2-g163: А впрочем is a concessive/transition phrase meaning 'but, however' or 'but, in fact' and opens a conciliatory remark. Впрочем here softens or concedes the previous point.
segments.p2-2-g164: Вы правы is a verbless predicative clause: вы (subject) + правы (short-form adjective used predicatively, plural to agree with вы) = 'you are right.' The semicolon separates this admission from the following confirmation.
segments.p2-2-g165: Действительно is an adverb meaning 'indeed' or 'actually', confirming the statement that follows; the comma prepares the emphatic final remark.
segments.p2-2-g166: И ... и is the correlative 'both ... and ...'. Пошло and подло are short/neuter predicative adjectives describing the action/behaviour as 'vulgar' (пошло, short form of пошлый) and 'base' or 'vile' (подло). Together they assert that the thing in question was both vulgar and mean. (Note: пошло here is an adjective meaning 'vulgar'; if listed senses treat it only as a verb, those verb senses do not apply in this context.)
segments.p2-2-g167: 'А' is a contrastive particle here (like 'and'/'but') introducing the remark; 'подлее' is the comparative form of 'подлый' ('more vile') used predicatively; 'всего' here means 'of all' (part of a superlative/comparative expression: 'the vilest of all'); 'то' is a demonstrative/introductory particle pointing forward to the subordinate clause ('the thing is that...').
segments.p2-2-g168: 'что' introduces the subordinate clause ('that ...'); 'я' is the subject; 'теперь' is the adverb 'now'; 'начал' is the past tense (masculine) of 'начать' + infinitive construction meaning 'began to ...'; 'перед вами' is the prepositional phrase with 'перед' + instrumental 'вами' meaning 'in front of / before you' (in your presence); 'оправдываться' is the reflexive infinitive 'to excuse/justify oneself' — together: 'that I have now begun to make excuses before you.'
segments.p2-2-g169: 'А' again is a connective particle ('and/but'); 'еще' intensifies the comparative here ('still' / 'even more'); 'подлее' is the comparative 'more base/viler'; 'то' is the demonstrative/introductory particle ('the fact is that...'). The whole phrase means 'and still more vile is (the fact)...'.
segments.p2-2-g170: 'что' introduces the clause; 'я' is the subject; 'делаю' is present tense 'I do / I am making' (1st person sing.); 'теперь' modifies the verb ('now'); 'это' is the demonstrative object 'this'; 'замечание' is the direct object 'remark' — literally 'that I am now making this remark.' Note the present-tense imperfective verb emphasizing the immediate action.
segments.p2-2-g171: 'Да' is an interjection/particle used to introduce or emphasize ('well/indeed'); 'довольно' here means 'enough' ('that's enough' / 'stop it'). Together they function as a brief command or dismissal: 'That's enough.'
segments.p2-2-g172: 'впрочем' is an adverbial linking particle meaning 'however / anyway,' softening or qualifying the previous interjection and connecting to what follows.
segments.p2-2-g173: 'а то' is a fixed colloquial sequence meaning 'otherwise' ('а' as adversative + 'то' forming the 'otherwise' sense); 'ведь' adds emphatic 'after all / you know'; 'никогда' is 'never'; 'и' here is an intensifying particle ('even'); 'не' negates the verb; 'кончишь' is 2nd person singular future/presentive of 'кончить' used rhetorically ('you won't finish / you'd never stop'). The whole gives the warning: 'otherwise you'd never finish.'
segments.p2-2-g174: 'все' = 'everything/all'; 'будет' is future of 'to be' ('will be'); 'одно другого' is a comparative idiom literally 'one of another' meaning successive comparison ('each one compared with the other'); 'подлее' is the comparative 'more base/viler' — together: 'everything will be one more vile than the next' i.e. 'everything will be getting more and more vile.'
segments.p2-2-g177: The temporal phrase 'Больше трех месяцев' = 'for more than three months.' 'я никак не в состоянии был' is a periphrastic expression: 'я был не в состоянии' ('I was not in a state/capable') with 'никак' adding 'in no way / simply could not.' Word order places the past auxiliary 'был' after the predicate phrase. 'сряду' ('in a row/continuously') modifies 'мечтать' ('to daydream'); 'и' links the clauses. 'начинал' (past imperfective 'began') + infinitive 'ощущать' ('to feel') indicates the onset of a feeling; 'непреодолимую потребность' is accusative 'an irresistible need'; 'ринуться в общество' is an infinitive phrase 'to rush/throw oneself into society' with 'в' indicating direction. Overall: inability to continue daydreaming leads to an urge to plunge into society.
segments.p2-2-g280: Был is the past tense of быть used impersonally with a time expression; here it functions like English 'it was' / 'it had been.' Почти is an adverb modifying год, meaning 'almost.' Год is a nominative time noun ('year') that, with был, yields the idiom 'Был почти год' = 'Almost a year had passed.'
segments.p2-2-g281: Как introduces a temporal subordinate clause meaning 'since/when' (here 'since'), marking the time point up to which the preceding statement holds. Я is the subject; последний раз is a fixed phrase 'the last time' (последний modifies раз). Перед тем is a small adverbial phrase 'before that / previously' that narrows 'последний раз' (together giving 'the last time before then'). Видел is the 1st-person singular past of видеть ('I saw' / 'I had seen') and takes Симонова as its object (the person seen).
segments.p2-2-g178: Ринуться is a perfective reflexive infinitive meaning 'to rush / throw oneself into' and here functions as the grammatical subject (an infinitive clause) of означало. В общество is the prepositional phrase 'into society' (в + accusative, motion/entry sense). Означало is the past neuter form of означать used impersonally with the infinitive subject ('meant, signified'). У меня is an idiomatic dative/possessive phrase meaning 'for me' (lit. 'at me'). Сходить в гости is an infinitive phrase meaning 'to go and pay a visit' (сходить = go briefly/visit and return), and к моему столоначальнику puts the visit toward the addressee in the dative: 'to my chief clerk / superior.'
segments.p2-2-g179: This is the dative apposition naming the person: Антону (dative of Антон) + the familiar patronymic Антонычу (colloquial form) + the surname Сеточкину in dative. All three are in dative because they complete 'сходить к ...' (to go to someone). The double name + patronymic with the colloquial ending gives a familiar, somewhat mocking tone.
segments.p2-2-g180: Это был is the simple past copula construction 'this/that was.' Единственный мой постоянный знакомый is a noun phrase where единственный ('the only') modifies мой постоянный знакомый ('my constant/steady acquaintance'); word order is normal for emphasis. Во всю мою жизнь uses во + accusative (всю мою жизнь) to indicate duration 'throughout my whole life.'
segments.p2-2-g181: И я даже сам удивляюсь is a present-tense, first-person clause: удивляюсь (imperfective) means 'I am surprised' or 'I marvel.' Даже сам is emphatic ('even I myself'). Теперь 'now' places the surprise in the present. Обстоятельству is in the dative because the verb удивляться (удивляюсь) governs the dative: 'to be surprised at/by this circumstance.'
segments.p2-2-g182: Но и к нему я ходил разве только тогда — 'But even to him I went, only then' — ходил is past imperfective ('used to go / would go') indicating a habitual or occasional past action. К нему is dative 'to him.' Разве только тогда is a restrictive phrase meaning 'only then' (разве modifies the restriction, giving a rhetorical sense).
segments.p2-2-g183: Когда уж наступала такая полоса — temporal subordinate 'when such a streak/period already set in.' Уж adds emphasis ('already' / 'indeed'). Наступала is imperfective past ('would begin / came on'). Полоса here is figurative: a 'span' or 'streak' (a run of feeling or fortune).
segments.p2-2-g184: А мечты мои доходили до такого счастия — 'and my dreams would reach such happiness.' Доходили до + genitive expresses 'to reach/up to' something; the slightly archaic form счастия is genitive of счастие (happiness). Мечты мои places the noun before the possessive for stylistic emphasis.
segments.p2-2-g185: Что надо было непременно и немедленно обняться с людьми и со всем человечеством — 'that it was necessary, without fail and immediately, to embrace people and all humanity.' Что introduces the result clause. Надо было is the impersonal past construction 'it was necessary' followed by the infinitive обняться ('to embrace' — reflexive perfective here, meaning to embrace one another). Указанные наречия (непременно, немедленно) intensify the necessity. С + instrumental marks accompaniment ('with people' / 'with all humanity').
segments.p2-2-g186: А для этого надо было иметь хоть одного человека в наличности — 'and for that it was necessary to have at least one person available/on hand.' Для этого 'for that / to that end.' Надо было иметь is again impersonal past 'one had to have.' Хоть одного человека means 'even/at least one person' (хоть here = 'at least'). В наличности literally 'in stock' = 'available / present.'
segments.p2-2-g187: Действительно существующего is a participial phrase modifying человека: действительно (adverb) = 'indeed/actually' and существующего is the present active participle (genitive singular masculine to agree with 'одного человека') of существовать, meaning 'existing' or 'actually existing.' The phrase stresses that the person must be real and present.
segments.p2-2-g188: К Антону Антонычу надо было — 'One had to (go) to Anton Antonovich.' К + dative marks the person to whom one goes. Надо было is the impersonal past ('it was necessary'), the verb that would complete the idea is continued in the next segment (являться). The comma shows the clause is interrupted for a parenthetical remark.
segments.p2-2-g189: Впрочем is a linking adverb meaning 'however' or 'in any case' and here softens or qualifies what follows (a concession/explanatory tone).
segments.p2-2-g190: Являться по вторникам (его день) — 'to show up / appear on Tuesdays (his day).' Являться here is an infinitive meaning 'to come / to present oneself.' По + plural (по вторникам) indicates repetition/frequency: 'on Tuesdays' (each Tuesday). The parenthetical 'его день' notes that Tuesday was the day associated with him.
segments.p2-2-g191: Следственно, is an adverb meaning 'consequently' or 'therefore,' linking the fact that one must go on Tuesdays to the conclusion in the next clause.
segments.p2-2-g192: И подгонять потребность обняться со всем человечеством надо было всегда ко вторнику — 'and so one had to arrange/adjust the urge to embrace all humanity always for (by) Tuesday.' Подгонять (infinitive) means 'to time/adjust/bring forward,' here applied to the noun потребность ('urge/need') + infinitive обняться ('to embrace'). Надо было всегда expresses repeated necessity ('one always had to'). Ко вторнику (к + dative) means 'toward/by Tuesday' — the intended day to carry out the visit/embrace.
segments.p2-2-g209: Больше + genitive (двух-трех гостей) expresses a quantity limitation: 'more than two or three guests.' The noun гостей is in genitive plural after больше; the phrase is an object/quantity phrase that will be completed by the main clause.
segments.p2-2-g210: и все тех же самых is an elliptical descriptive tag modifying the guests: и = 'and'; все = 'all' (referring to the whole set); тех (genitive plural of тот) + же (emphatic particle) + самых (самый in plural, here 'the very/exact') together mean 'all of the very same ones.' The phrase is elliptical (the noun 'guests' is omitted but understood).
segments.p2-2-g211: я (subject) + никогда (ever/never) + там (there) + не видывал (past of the iterative/ habitual verb видывать with negation). Не видывал is a past imperfective/iterative form meaning 'did not (ever) see' or 'never saw' (habitually/at any time). Word order is straightforward subject–adverbials–predicate.
segments.p2-2-g212: Толковали is a past-tense plural verb with an implicit subject ('they' or 'people') meaning 'they were discoursing/expounding/talking over.' Про + noun = 'about/regarding' and акциз is the topic 'excise (tax).' The clause is one item in a list of topics discussed.
segments.p2-2-g213: про торги в Сенате: про = 'about'; торги (plural) here means 'dealings/bargains/trading' and в Сенате = 'in the Senate.' The preposition в takes the locative/ prepositional case (Сенате) indicating where those dealings took place.
segments.p2-2-g214: о жалованье: о = 'about/concerning'; жалованье (old/colloquial form) means 'salary' (what they were discussing). The noun is in the prepositional case after о.
segments.p2-2-g215: о производстве: о = 'about'; производство here names another official topic. (In bureaucratic contexts производство can mean official proceedings or matters of promotion/administration.) The noun is used with о to list a subject of talk.
segments.p2-2-g216: о его превосходительстве: о = 'about'; его = 'his' (referring to a titled person) and превосходительстве (an honorific noun) = 'His Excellency.' The whole phrase means 'about His Excellency' as another topic of conversation.
segments.p2-2-g217: о средстве нравиться и проч.: о = 'about'; средство = 'a means/method'; нравиться here is infinitive 'to please' (i.e., a means to be liked); и проч. is short for 'и прочее' = 'and so on.' The construction lists yet another subject — 'ways of being liked' — before the closing 'etc.'
segments.p2-2-g218: This segment is a punctuation mark (a comma) continuing the previous list; it has no lexical items to analyze.
segments.p2-2-g219: и проч. is an abbreviated fixed phrase (и прочее) meaning 'and so forth' or 'etc.' It closes the list of topics; и = 'and' and проч. = 'etc.'
segments.p2-2-g220: Я имел терпение + infinitive construction: 'I had the patience to ...' Имел терпение (past tense) is followed by two infinitives (высиживать ... и их слушать). Высиживать подле этих людей дураком часа по четыре = 'to sit out/put up with being beside these people as a fool for about four hours at a stretch'; подле = 'beside/near'; дураком is instrumental case indicating the role assumed 'as a fool'; часа по четыре expresses duration 'about four hours each time' with по + numeral meaning 'per/each.' Их слушать = 'and to listen to them' (их is the accusative 'them').
segments.p2-2-g221: сам не смея и не умея ни об чем с ними заговорить is a pair of deverbial participles/gerunds describing the speaker's incapacity: сам (emphatic 'I myself') + не смея ('not daring') and не умея ('not knowing how') govern the infinitive заговорить ('to begin/speechify'). Ни об чем ('about nothing' in negative contexts = 'about anything') uses ни...чем to strengthen the negation; с ними = 'with them' (instrumental). The whole phrase means he neither dared nor knew how to start talking with them about anything.
segments.p2-2-g193: Помещался is the past reflexive/intransitive form indicating location or residence ('was situated' / 'lived'). The verb precedes the subject here (Помещался этот Антон Антоныч), which is a permissible inverted word order that slightly foregrounds the verb. У + genitive (у Пяти углов) gives the place 'by/at' and here names the location ('Five Corners').
segments.p2-2-g194: в + locative (в четвертом этаже) indicates the floor ('on the fourth floor'). The coordinated phrase и в четырех комнатках repeats the preposition and uses a numeral + noun phrase to mean 'in four little rooms'; the forms reflect standard Russian agreement with numerals and the diminutive комнатках.
segments.p2-2-g195: низеньких and the following expression мал мала меньше are adjectival descriptions agreeing with комнатках (diminutive plural). The phrase мал мала меньше is a colloquial/idiomatic, repetitive intensifier meaning 'very small' or 'ever so small' and is stylistically emphatic and informal.
segments.p2-2-g196: имевших is a past active participle (agreeing with the feminine/diminutive plural noun it modifies) meaning 'having' or 'possessing' and introduces a participial phrase that describes the rooms. самый here is an intensifier ('most' / 'utterly'), followed by adjectives (экономический, желтенький) that describe вид ('appearance'); вид is the noun governed by имевших ('having the most economical and yellowish appearance').
segments.p2-2-g197: Были у него is an existential/possessive construction literally 'there were at him' but idiomatically 'he had.' The order (Были у него две дочери) places the existential verb first. Две дочери and их тетка are the items that exist/are possessed.
segments.p2-2-g198: разливавшая is a past active participle in the feminine singular that attaches to тетка and functions like a relative clause: 'their aunt, who poured (served) tea.' Чай is the direct object of разливавшая.
segments.p2-2-g199: Дочкам here begins a topicalized/indirect construction; the core is Одной было тринадцать where the dative однОй + impersonal было expresses age: 'one (of them) was thirteen (years old)'. Russian commonly uses dative with 'было' to state someone's age (ему было тринадцать).
segments.p2-2-g200: а другой четырнадцать лет is an elliptical clause: the verb (было) is omitted but understood — 'and the other (was) fourteen years old.' Четырнадцать лет is the standard numeral + genitive construction for ages.
segments.p2-2-g201: обе были uses обе ('both') with the past plural были to mean 'both were.' Курносенькие is a diminutive, affectionate adjective ('snub‑nosed/cute') in nominative plural agreeing with обе.
segments.p2-2-g202: и я их ужасно конфузился: конфузился is a reflexive imperfective past verb meaning 'was embarrassed' or 'felt awkward.' Их is the accusative plural pronoun 'them' (I was embarrassed by them), and ужасно is an intensifier 'terribly/very.'
segments.p2-2-g203: потому что is the causal conjunction 'because.' Они все шептались про себя uses the reciprocal reflexive шептались ('whispered to one another'); про себя here means 'to themselves/among themselves' (a private murmuring). И хихикали is a simple past clause 'and giggled.'
segments.p2-2-g204: Хозяин сидел обыкновенно в кабинете is a straightforward past habitual statement: хозяин ('the host') is the subject, сидел ('sat / used to sit') is past tense, and обыкновенно ('usually') qualifies the habitual nature. В кабинете uses в + locative to indicate the study/office.
segments.p2-2-g205: на кожаном диване uses на + locative/in to indicate position 'on the leather sofa'; кожаном is an adjective in the locative agreeing with диване.
segments.p2-2-g206: перед столом uses the preposition перед + instrumental (столом) to indicate position 'in front of the table.'
segments.p2-2-g207: вместе с is the fixed expression 'together with' taking an instrumental complement. каким-нибудь седым гостем is an indefinite phrase meaning 'some gray-haired guest,' with каким-нибудь providing indefiniteness and седым + гостем in the instrumental singular.
segments.p2-2-g208: чиновником is instrumental (linked to вместе с) meaning 'an official'; из нашего или даже из постороннего ведомства is a genitive-phrased modifier 'from our or even from an outside/different department,' where из + genitive names origin and даже ('even') adds emphasis.
segments.p2-2-g222: Я is the nominative first‑person pronoun 'I'. Тупел is the past imperfective of тупеть and describes a gradual or progressive change (I grew dull/slow‑witted); the imperfective past here presents an ongoing state or repeated tendency rather than a single completed event.
segments.p2-2-g223: По нескольку раз is an adverbial phrase meaning 'several times' or 'repeatedly' (по + numeral form gives a distributive/repetitive sense). Принимался (imperfective, reflexive) + infinitive expresses repeatedly beginning to do something, so принимался потеть = 'would begin to sweat' (an iterative past action). Потеть is literal 'to sweat' (perspire).
segments.p2-2-g224: Надо мной is a prepositional phrase meaning 'over me' / 'upon me' (classical surface form nádo мной from над + мной); носился is the imperfective past of носиться used figuratively here to mean something ran around/over or prevailed about the speaker; паралич is used figuratively as 'paralysis' (a state that seizes one).
segments.p2-2-g225: Но links contrast ('but'). Это было is a simple past copular clause 'this/it was'. Хорошо and полезно are predicative adverb/adjective forms meaning 'good' and 'useful/beneficial'; и simply connects the two qualities.
segments.p2-2-g226: Возвратясь is a perfective gerund (деепричастие) from возвратиться meaning 'having returned' and sets the temporal background for the main clause. Домой is a directional adverb meaning 'home' or 'to home'.
segments.p2-2-g227: Я is the subject. На некоторое время is an adverbial phrase 'for some time' (на + accusative indicating duration). Откладывал is the imperfective past 'postponed' or 'put off' (habitual/continuous past). Мое желание обняться со всем человечеством is the direct object: 'my desire to embrace/by embracing all humanity' — обняться is the reflexive infinitive 'to embrace' (can be literal or figurative), and со + творительный marks 'with' or 'together with' all humanity.
segments.p2-2-g228: Был is the past tense of быть used existentially ('there was' / 'there existed'). In this sentence it introduces the existence of someone in the speaker's past.
segments.p2-2-g229: Впрочем is a parenthetical discourse marker meaning 'however' or 'besides/anyway', softening or qualifying the previous statement.
segments.p2-2-g230: У меня expresses possession ('I had' / 'there was with me'). И ещё как будто is a cluster of particles: и ещё adds 'even/also/another', and как будто ('as if') introduces a tentative or half‑ironical qualification. Знакомый here means 'an acquaintance' (a person the speaker knew).
segments.p2-2-g231: Симонов is a proper name (surname) in apposition to 'one acquaintance' — it identifies the person.
segments.p2-2-g232: Бывший is a past participle meaning 'former' and modifies the noun phrase that follows. Мой школьный товарищ literally 'my school companion' (школьный = relating to school; товарищ = companion/mate), i.e. a former schoolmate.
segments.p2-2-g233: Школьных товарищей is the genitive plural 'school companions' (schoolmates). У меня было is the existential/possessive construction 'I had' / 'there were with me' in the past (было), so the phrase means 'I had schoolmates' or 'there were schoolmates of mine'.
segments.p2-2-g234: Пожалуй is a modal particle meaning 'probably' or 'one might say', marking some uncertainty or modesty about the statement.
segments.p2-2-g235: И много in this context means 'quite many' or 'indeed many' (и functions as an intensifier here). В Петербурге locates the group 'in Petersburg' (preposition в + locative).
segments.p2-2-g236: Но introduces the contrast 'but'. Я с ними не водился uses водиться (imperfective) with the instrumental (с ними) to mean 'to associate with' or 'keep company (with them)' and the negation не indicates he did not do so. И даже перестал expresses 'and even ceased' — перестал + infinitive (кланяться) means 'stopped (doing something)'. На улице is a locative/directional phrase 'in the street' and кланяться means 'to bow' (here 'bow to them' in public).
segments.p2-2-g237: "Я," is the first-person singular nominative pronoun serving as the sentence subject; the following comma sets it off as the speaker's self-reference before the parenthetical phrase.
segments.p2-2-g238: "может быть," is a fixed parenthetical expression meaning "perhaps/maybe"; here it qualifies the whole statement and is formed from the modal root "может" (possibility) plus "быть" (to be).
segments.p2-2-g239: This chunk contains the verb phrase and destination: "и" (= also/even) adds emphasis; "на службу-то" uses the accusative phrase with "на" to mean "into service/into the civil service," with enclitic "-то" adding contrastive emphasis; "в другое ведомство" specifies the target department ("в" + accusative = into); "перешел" is the past tense perfective verb "transferred/switched"; "для того" begins a purpose construction that leads into the following infinitival clause.
segments.p2-2-g240: "чтоб" (= чтобы) introduces the purpose clause "in order to"; "не быть вместе с ними" is an infinitival clause meaning "not to be together with them"; the conjunction "и" links a second coordinated purpose "разом отрезать" where "разом" (= "at once/all together") modifies "отрезать" (to cut off); "со всем ненавистным моим детством" uses the preposition "со" + instrumental to indicate that he severs himself together with his "whole hateful childhood" (the adjective-before-possessive word order is literary/stilted).
segments.p2-2-g241: "Проклятие на эту школу," is an exclamatory imprecation: the noun "Проклятие" (curse) followed by the preposition "на" plus the accusative "эту школу" (this school) expresses "a curse upon this school."
segments.p2-2-g242: This continues the imprecation by repeating the preposition "на" + demonstrative "эти" and a string of adjectives "ужасные каторжные" modifying "годы" (years); "кaторжные" specifically means "penal/convict" so the phrase targets those grueling years.
segments.p2-2-g243: "Одним словом," is a formulaic linking phrase (literally "in one word") meaning "in short" or "to sum up," built with the instrumental "Одним" + "словом."
segments.p2-2-g244: "с товарищами" (with comrades) is the prepositional/instrumental phrase marking the people concerned; "я" is the subject; "тотчас же" is an adverbial expression meaning "immediately" with "же" as an emphatic particle; "разошелся" is the past reflexive verb "to part/part company," describing that he broke off relations.
segments.p2-2-g245: "как вышел на волю." — here "как" introduces a temporal clause meaning "as/when/as soon as"; "вышел на волю" is an idiomatic perfective past construction meaning "went out into freedom/was released" ("воля" = freedom).
segments.p2-2-g246: "Оставались" is the imperfective past (3rd person plural) meaning "remained/were left," describing that two or three persons were left over; "два-три человека" is the numeric expression "two or three people" ("человека" is the genitive form used with numerals).
segments.p2-2-g247: "с которыми" is the instrumental plural relative pronoun "with whom" introducing the subordinate clause; "я" is the subject; "еще" means "still/yet"; "кланялся" is the imperfective past reflexive verb "would bow/bowed (pay respects)," implying a habitual or continued action.
segments.p2-2-g248: "встречаясь." is a present adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning "meeting/when meeting" and indicates that the bowing occurred in the circumstance of meeting each other (simultaneous action).
segments.p2-2-g249: "В том числе" is a fixed idiom meaning "among them/included"; here the past tense verb "был" states existence/placement, and the particle "и" functions as "also/too" (emphasizing inclusion) before the name "Симонов." The phrase order places the idiom up front for emphasis: "Among them was also Simonov."
segments.p2-2-g250: Relative clause introduced by "который" (who). "в школе у нас" is a locative expression meaning "in our school/among us at school," and "ничем не отличался" uses the instrumental "ничем" with negation to mean "did not differ in anything / did not stand out at all." The verb "отличался" is imperfective past, indicating habitual/characteristic lack of distinction.
segments.p2-2-g251: Predicate with past tense copula "был" followed by short-form adjectives "ровен и тих" (short predicative forms) meaning "steady/even (even‑tempered) and quiet." Short adjective forms after "быть" state a temporary or characteristic property.
segments.p2-2-g252: "Но" introduces contrast. "в нем" (in him) is the locative phrase referring back to Simonov; "я отличил" (I distinguished/noticed) is a past action (perfective here indicating a specific perception) taking the direct object(s) "некоторую независимость характера и даже честность" — "a certain independence of character and even honesty," where "некоторую" modifies "независимость" and "даже" adds emphasis to "честность."
segments.p2-2-g253: "Даже не думаю" is a colloquial negated present-tense clause meaning "I don't even think (so)." "Даже" (even) adds emphasis to the negation; "думаю" is 1sg present of "думать."
segments.p2-2-g254: Subordinate clause introduced by "что" (that). "он был ограничен" is a past-state description with the short-form adjective "ограничен" meaning "limited/narrow‑minded;" "очень уж" is a colloquial intensifier combining "очень" (very) and "уж" (indeed/really) to strengthen the adjective.
segments.p2-2-g255: "У меня" here expresses having/experiencing (literally "at me/for me"), so "У меня с ним бывали" means "I used to have/had with him." The verb "бывали" is imperfective plural/past used for habitual or occasional occurrences, and "когда-то" (split here) means "once/at one time/at times;" "довольно светлые минуты" = "rather bright/cheerful moments."
segments.p2-2-g256: Contrast introduced by "но" (but). "недолго продолжались" = "did not last long" ("недолго" adverb + imperfective "продолжались"). "и как-то вдруг" is a colloquial sequence meaning "and somehow suddenly," and "задернулись туманом" uses the reflexive verb "задернулись" with the instrumental "туманом" to mean "were drawn/veiled in fog" (i.e., became obscured).
segments.p2-2-g257: Simple nominative pronoun subject "Он," standing alone before parenthetical remarks; it marks who is being spoken about.
segments.p2-2-g258: "видимо" is an adverb meaning "apparently/it would seem," used parenthetically to indicate the narrator's inference about the subject.
segments.p2-2-g259: "тяготился" is imperfective past meaning "was burdened by/was weighed down by" and governs the instrumental "этими воспоминаниями" (by these memories). The coordinating "и" links this clause to the following remark about his fear.
segments.p2-2-g260: "кажется" (it seems) is another parenthetical adverb here, softening the statement that follows and marking it as the speaker's impression.
segments.p2-2-g261: "все боялся" is colloquial: "все" here functions as an adverbial particle meaning something like "still/continually" (i.e., he kept fearing), and "боялся" is the imperfective past "was afraid/feared." Together they mean "he continually feared" or "he kept on fearing."
segments.p2-2-g262: Subordinate clause after "что" (that). "я впаду" is 1st person singular future of perfective "впасть" meaning "I will fall into/return to"; "в прежний тон" uses "в" + accusative to indicate entering a prior state or manner — "into my former tone/way of speaking."
segments.p2-2-g263: Main clause: 'Я подозревал' uses the past tense imperfective verb 'подозревал' (masculine singular) meaning 'I suspected' or 'I had a suspicion.' The clause is finished with a comma because it introduces the subordinate clause beginning with 'что'.
segments.p2-2-g264: Subordinate clause introduced by the conjunction 'что' ('that'). 'я был' is past tense of 'быть' (to be). The predicate is the short-form adjective 'противен' with the experiencer in the dative 'ему' — a common Russian construction meaning 'I was odious/repulsive to him.' 'очень' intensifies the adjective.
segments.p2-2-g265: 'Но все-таки' is a concessive particle meaning 'but/nevertheless/after all.' 'ходил' is the past imperfective of 'ходить' here indicating repeated or habitual motion (or simply that he went despite his feeling). 'к нему' is the directional prepositional phrase 'to him' (dative).
segments.p2-2-g266: This is an adverbial participial/ adjectival phrase modifying the speaker (implied 'я'): 'не уверенный в том' = 'not sure about that.' 'наверно' is a parenthetical adverb 'probably' or 'I suppose,' softening the statement. Word order places this qualifying phrase after the main clause.
segments.p2-2-g267: 'Вот однажды' is an introductory temporal phrase meaning 'one day' or 'once' with 'вот' as a discourse marker ('well/now') giving immediacy to the recollection.
segments.p2-2-g268: 'в четверг' is a simple temporal adverbial 'on Thursday' (the preposition 'в' used for time). The comma separates parenthetical adverbials.
segments.p2-2-g269: Two adverbial participles (деепричастия) give the reasons: 'не выдержав' (perfective past gerund) = 'not having borne/able to endure' and 'зная' (present gerund) = 'and knowing.' Together they indicate motive or circumstance for the main action.
segments.p2-2-g270: A subordinate clause introduced by 'что' stating a fact the speaker knew: 'в четверг у Антона Антоныча дверь заперта.' 'у' + name in genitive indicates 'at Anton Antonovich's (place),' and 'дверь заперта' uses a short-form adjective/ passive participle to mean 'the door is locked.'
segments.p2-2-g271: Main clause: 'я вспомнил' is perfective past of 'вспомнить' — 'I remembered/ recalled.' 'о Симонове' uses the prepositional to indicate whom he remembered.
segments.p2-2-g272: 'Подымаясь' is a present adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning 'ascending/while going up.' It expresses an action simultaneous with the main clause. 'к нему в четвертый этаж' is a directional phrase: 'to his (place) on the fourth floor' with 'в' + accusative ('в четвертый этаж') indicating movement into/onto that floor.
segments.p2-2-g273: 'я именно думал о том' — 'I was exactly/precisely thinking of the fact that...' 'именно' emphasizes that the thought was specifically about the following point. 'о том' points forward to the subordinate clause.
segments.p2-2-g274: Subordinate content: two 'that' clauses. 'тяготится мною' uses the reflexive verb 'тяготиться' with the agent/causer in the instrumental (мною) to mean 'is burdened/affected by me.' The second part 'напрасно я это иду' is colloquial: 'напрасно' = 'in vain,' 'я ... иду' (present imperfective) means 'I am going (there) for nothing,' where 'это' refers to the visit.
segments.p2-2-g275: 'Но так как' is the causal conjunction 'but/since/as.' 'кончалось всегда тем' is the idiomatic phrase 'it always ended (up) with the fact that...' where 'тем' introduces the content clause that follows ('тем, что...'). The verb 'кончалось' is imperfective past, indicating a habitual/resulting outcome.
segments.p2-2-g276: 'что подобные соображения' begins the content clause: 'that such considerations...' 'подобные соображения' is the nominative subject of the verb in the following clause.
segments.p2-2-g277: 'как нарочно' is a parenthetical adverbial phrase meaning 'as if intentionally' or 'as though on purpose,' set off by commas to show the tone of annoyance or irony.
segments.p2-2-g278: 'еще более подбивали меня лезть в двусмысленное положение' — 'they urged me even more to get into an ambiguous position.' 'подбивали' (past plural) agrees with 'соображения'; 'меня' is the object; 'лезть' is an infinitive meaning 'to get into/force one's way into' an undesirable situation; 'двусмысленное положение' = 'an ambiguous/dubious situation.'
segments.p2-2-g279: 'то я и вошел' is the result clause: the particle 'то' with the demonstrative force gives 'so/then,' 'я и вошел' — 'I therefore went in.' 'вошел' is perfective past ('entered').
segments.p2-2-g100: 'Но' is an adversative/exclamatory particle here (softening/introducing an exclamation). 'Сколько любви' is a quantity exclamation: 'how much love' with 'любви' in the genitive after 'сколько'. The word order foregrounds the quantity for rhetorical effect.
segments.p2-2-g101: Vocative/exclamatory interjection: 'господи' = 'Lord!' used to intensify the emotion of the exclamation; grammatically a standalone interjection (not inflected here).
segments.p2-2-g102: 'переживал' is the imperfective past tense, 1st person singular (I used to experience/I experienced repeatedly). Word order ('сколько любви переживал я') places the subject 'я' after the verb for emphasis — typical of expressive Russian.
segments.p2-2-g103: Parenthetical adverb 'бывало' marks habitual or characteristic occurrences — 'used to happen / it would be the case' — and here signals that the experiencing of love was something that occurred in those dreams.
segments.p2-2-g104: Prepositional phrase: 'в' + locative plural 'мечтах' = 'in those dreams'; 'этих' points to particular dreams and 'моих' is the possessive 'of mine' placed after the noun (poetic/emphatic word order).
segments.p2-2-g105: 'в этих "спасеньях ..."' — the first 'в' governs the locative plural 'спасеньях' ('in these salvations/redemptions'); the embedded phrase uses 'во' + accusative 'во все прекрасное и высокое' indicating movement/toward or orientation 'into/for all that is beautiful and lofty' (idiomatic, figurative).
segments.p2-2-g106: 'хоть и' is a concessive formula meaning 'even if / although'; 'фантастической любви' is genitive, describing the kind of love (an unreal/fantastical love). The 'и' here strengthens the concessive 'хоть'.
segments.p2-2-g107: A long participial clause modifying 'любви' (feminine): 'хоть и никогда ни к чему человеческому на деле не прилагавшейся' = 'even if it never, not even to anything human, was applied in practice.' Note the doubled negative particles ('никогда ни ... не') for emphasis and 'на деле' = 'in reality.' 'Прилагавшейся' is a past passive/reflective participle agreeing with 'любви'.
segments.p2-2-g108: 'Но' introduces contrast ('but'); the idiom 'до того' here means 'to such an extent/so much'; 'было ее много' is an impersonal/existential construction 'there was much of it' with 'ее' (of it) referring back to 'love.'
segments.p2-2-g109: Simple noun phrase in genitive 'этой любви' — 'of this love' — repeating and specifying the referent for emphasis.
segments.p2-2-g110: 'что' is the result/consequence conjunction 'that' (introducing a result clause), 'потом' = 'later/afterwards.'
segments.p2-2-g111: Adverbial phrase 'на деле' meaning 'in practice / in fact'; 'на' is the preposition used in the idiom and 'деле' is the noun 'matter/thing' here functioning as 'in reality.'
segments.p2-2-g112: Impersonal construction: 'уж и потребности даже не ощущалось ее прилагать' — literally 'already even the need was not felt to apply it.' 'Ощущалось' is impersonal passive 'was felt,' and the infinitive 'прилагать' ('to apply') takes 'ее' (it) as its object; 'даже' is emphatic 'even.'
segments.p2-2-g113: Elliptical final clause with inverted word order: 'излишняя б уж это роскошь была.' 'Излишняя' (fem. nom.) 'superfluous'; 'б' is the reduced conditional particle 'бы' (would/have been) attached before the finite verb 'была' to form a counterfactual/conditional nuance; 'уж' is an intensifier; 'это роскошь была' = 'this would have been a luxury.'
segments.p2-2-g114: The pronoun 'Все' here functions as the subject meaning 'everything' or 'all of it' — the generalizing subject of the sentence.
segments.p2-2-g115: Parenthetical particle 'впрочем' = 'however / nonetheless,' used to moderate or qualify the preceding statement.
segments.p2-2-g116: 'преблагополучно' is an intensive adverb ('very/hyper-prosperously'); 'всегда' = 'always'; 'оканчивалось' is past/imperfective passive/impersonal 'ended' (it ended); the instrument 'ленивым и упоительным переходом' (instrumental case) indicates 'by a lazy and exhilarating transition' and 'к искусству' is the goal 'to art.'
segments.p2-2-g117: 'То есть' is the explanatory phrase 'that is,' and 'к прекрасным формам бытия' is a dative phrase 'to/into the beautiful forms of being/existence' (forms of existence in dative plural).
segments.p2-2-g118: 'Совсем готовым' is an adjectival phrase 'completely ready' (agreeing in case/number with 'формам' earlier — dative plural). The adverb 'совсем' intensifies 'готовым.'
segments.p2-2-g119: A sequence of past passive participles (agreeing with 'формам' in case/number): 'сильно украденным у поэтов и романистов' = 'largely/strongly stolen from poets and novelists,' and 'приспособленным ко всевозможным услугам и требованиям' = 'adapted to all sorts of services and demands.' 'У' indicates source/agent in the sense 'from/at the hands of,' and 'ко' is the preposition variant used before certain words meaning 'to/toward.'
segments.p2-2-g120: 'Я' is the pronoun subject 'I' (stressed by following comma) — introduces the speaker's claim about himself.
segments.p2-2-g121: 'например' = 'for example / for instance,' a parenthetical adverb showing the claim is an instance of the pattern previously described.
segments.p2-2-g122: 'над' + instrumental 'всеми' expresses superiority/dominance ('over everyone'); 'торжествую' is present 1st person singular 'I triumph' or 'I exult.'
