segments.p2-2-g123: Short nominal 'все' is the resumed subject/object 'everyone/everything' — here continuing the description of others' reaction to the speaker.
segments.p2-2-g124: 'разумеется' = 'of course' — parenthetical indicating the following is to be taken as obvious/expected.
segments.p2-2-g125: 'во прахе' is an idiom 'in the dust' (humiliated); 'и принуждены' ('and are forced') is a predicative participle phrase referring back to 'все'; 'добровольно признать' = 'to voluntarily admit/acknowledge' and 'все мои совершенства' is the accusative object 'all my perfections.'
segments.p2-2-g126: 'А' is a contrastive conjunction here ('and/yet'); 'я' is the subject; 'всех их' (accusative) = 'all of them'; 'прощаю' is present 1st person 'I forgive.' The clause closes the boastful picture with the speaker's magnanimity.
segments.p2-2-g127: 'Я влюбляюсь' — present tense, reflexive verb 'влюбляться' in 1st person singular: 'I fall in love' (ongoing/habitual or immediate action depending on context).
segments.p2-2-g128: Gerundial construction 'будучи' (from 'быть') = 'being'; the following nouns are in the instrumental ('знаменитым поэтом и камергером') to express the state or role the speaker occupies while the action occurs.
segments.p2-2-g129: A sequence of coordinated actions in the present tense: 'получаю несметные миллионы' ('I receive countless millions'), 'и тотчас же жертвую их на род человеческий' ('and immediately donate them to the human race'), 'и тут же исповедываюсь перед всем народом в моих позорах' ('and immediately confess before the whole people about my shames'). Note particles 'тотчас же'/'тут же' emphasize immediacy; 'исповедываюсь' is reflexive 'I confess' (often with a moral/religious overtone).
segments.p2-2-g130: 'Которые' is the relative pronoun 'which' (introducing the relative clause referring back to 'позорах'); it agrees in number/gender with the antecedent and heads the clause.
segments.p2-2-g131: Parenthetical 'разумеется' = 'of course,' marking the following as obvious or expected (tone-marker).
segments.p2-2-g132: 'Не просто позоры' = 'not merely shames' — 'не просто' negates/suspends the literal reading and signals that these 'позоры' have additional, more elevated content.
segments.p2-2-g133: 'А' introduces the contrast 'but'; 'заключают в себе' (3rd pl. present) = 'contain in themselves'; 'чрезвычайно много' = 'an extraordinarily large amount'; '"прекрасного и высокого"' are substantivized adjectives in genitive expressing 'the beautiful and the lofty.'
segments.p2-2-g134: 'Чего-то' is an indefinite/partitive phrase 'something (of)', formed with the genitive 'чего' + particle 'то' = 'something'; 'манфредовского' is an adjectival genitive meaning 'Manfredian' or 'of Manfred' (i.e., reminiscent of Manfred — a literary reference).

sentences.p2-2-s1: But the streak of my libertine days came to an end, and I began to feel terribly nauseous.
sentences.p2-2-s2: Repentance set in, and I chased it away: it was excessively sickening.
sentences.p2-2-s3: Little by little I, however, also grew accustomed to it.
sentences.p2-2-s4: I became accustomed to everything, that is, not so much that I 'got used' to it as that I somehow voluntarily agreed to endure it.
sentences.p2-2-s5: But I had an escape, one that reconciled everything — that was to take refuge in "all that is beautiful and lofty," of course, in dreams.
sentences.p2-2-s6: I dreamed terribly; I dreamed for three months on end, having burrowed into my corner, and believe me, in those moments I was nothing like that gentleman who, in the agitation of a chicken's heart, sewed a German beaver onto the collar of his greatcoat.
sentences.p2-2-s8: I wouldn't even have let my ten-vershok lieutenant come visit me then.
sentences.p2-2-s9: I couldn't even imagine him then.
sentences.p2-2-s10: What my dreams were like and how I could be content with them — it's hard to say that now, but then I was content with them.
sentences.p2-2-s11: However, even now I am partly satisfied with them.
sentences.p2-2-s12: Dreams came to me especially sweeter and stronger after the libertine; they came with repentance and tears, with curses and raptures.
sentences.p2-2-s31: Everyone cries and kisses me (otherwise what would they be but blockheads), while I go barefoot and hungry to preach new ideas and I smash reactionaries at Austerlitz.
sentences.p2-2-s32: Then a march is played, an amnesty is granted, the Pope agrees to leave Rome for Brazil; then a ball for all Italy at the Villa Borghese, which is on the shore of Lake Como, since Lake Como is deliberately moved to Rome for that occasion; then a scene in the bushes, etc.
sentences.p2-2-s17: Either a hero or filth — there was no middle ground.
sentences.p2-2-s18: That was precisely what ruined me, because in the filth I comforted myself with the thought that at other times I am a hero, and the hero covered up the filth: to the ordinary man, they say, it is shameful to soil himself, while the hero is too lofty to be completely sullied — consequently, it is permissible to get dirty.
sentences.p2-2-s19: It's remarkable that these surges of "all that is beautiful and lofty" came to me even during my debauchery, and precisely then, when I was already at the very bottom, they came in such a way — as separate little flashes, as if reminding me of themselves, yet they did not, however, destroy the debauchery by their appearance; on the contrary, they seemed to keep it alive by contrast and came exactly as much as was needed for a good sauce.
sentences.p2-2-s20: The sauce here consisted of contradiction and suffering, of an agonizing inner analysis, and all those torments and little torments imparted a certain piquancy, even a meaning to my debauchery; in a word, they quite fulfilled the role of a good sauce.
sentences.p2-2-s21: All this was not without a certain depth.
sentences.p2-2-s22: And could I possibly have agreed to be a simple, vulgar, straightforward, scribal lecher and to bear all that filth on myself?
sentences.p2-2-s23: What, then, could have enticed me in it and lured me out into the street at night?
sentences.p2-2-s24: No — I had a noble loophole for everything.
sentences.p2-2-s37: You will say that it is tawdry and base to put all this now on the market, after so many raptures and tears, which I myself confessed to.
sentences.p2-2-s13: There were moments of such sheer rapture, such happiness, that not even the slightest mockery was felt within me, I swear to God.
sentences.p2-2-s14: There was faith, hope, and love.
sentences.p2-2-s15: That's exactly it: I blindly believed then that by some miracle, by some external circumstance, all this would suddenly part and expand; that a horizon of appropriate activity would suddenly present itself — beneficial, beautiful and, above all, entirely ready (what exactly — I never knew, but the important thing is — entirely ready) — and then I would suddenly step out into the light of day, almost as if on a white horse and even in a laurel wreath.
sentences.p2-2-s16: I couldn't even grasp a secondary role, and that's precisely why, in fact, I very calmly occupied the last one.
sentences.p2-2-s39: Do you really think that I am ashamed of all this, and that all of this was more foolish than anything at all in your lives, gentlemen?
sentences.p2-2-s40: And besides, believe me, that I actually had something that was put together quite well.
sentences.p2-2-s43: Not everything, after all, happened on Lake Como.
sentences.p2-2-s44: But, in fact, you are right; indeed, it was both vulgar and base.
sentences.p2-2-s45: And the vilest thing of all is that I have now begun to make excuses before you.
sentences.p2-2-s46: And still more base is the fact that I am now making this remark.
sentences.p2-2-s47: All right, that's enough, anyway; otherwise you'd never finish: everything will become more and more vile.
sentences.p2-2-s50: For more than three months I simply couldn't daydream in a row, and I began to feel an irresistible need to rush into society.
sentences.p2-2-s79: It had been almost a year since I last saw Simonov.
sentences.p2-2-s51: For me, 'rushing into society' meant going to call on my chief clerk, Anton Antonovich Setochkin.
sentences.p2-2-s52: He was my only steady acquaintance throughout my whole life, and even I am now surprised by that circumstance.
sentences.p2-2-s53: But I went to see him only, truly, when such a spell set in, and my dreams reached such happiness that one absolutely and immediately had to embrace people and all humanity; and for that one had to have at least one person actually existing on hand.
sentences.p2-2-s54: One had to present oneself to Anton Antonovich, however, on Tuesdays (his day); consequently, one always had to time the urge to embrace all humanity for Tuesday.
sentences.p2-2-s59: I never saw more than two or three guests there, and they were always the very same ones.
sentences.p2-2-s60: They discoursed about the excise, about bargains in the Senate, about salary, about proceedings, about His Excellency, about means of being liked, and so forth.
sentences.p2-2-s62: I had the patience to sit beside these people for about four hours at a time, acting the fool, and to listen to them, not daring and not knowing how to begin a conversation with them about anything.
sentences.p2-2-s55: This Anton Antonych lived at the Five Corners, on the fourth floor and in four little rooms, low and ever so small, which had the most economical and yellowish appearance.
sentences.p2-2-s56: He had two daughters and their aunt, who poured the tea.
sentences.p2-2-s57: The daughters — one was thirteen and the other fourteen years old; both were snub-nosed, and I was terribly embarrassed by them, because they all whispered to themselves and giggled.
sentences.p2-2-s58: The host usually sat in his study, on the leather sofa, in front of the table, together with some gray-haired guest, an official from our department or even from a different one.
sentences.p2-2-s63: I grew dull; several times I would begin to sweat; a paralysis ran over me; but that was good and useful.
sentences.p2-2-s64: Returning home, I postponed my desire to embrace all humanity for a time.
sentences.p2-2-s65: There was, however, as it were, one more acquaintance of mine — Simonov, a former school companion of mine.
sentences.p2-2-s66: I probably had many schoolmates in Petersburg, but I didn't associate with them and even stopped bowing to them in the street.
sentences.p2-2-s67: I, perhaps, even transferred into the service of another department so as not to be with them and to cut myself off at once along with my whole hateful childhood.
sentences.p2-2-s68: A curse on this school, on these terrible, penal years!
sentences.p2-2-s69: In short, I immediately parted ways with my comrades as soon as I was set free.
sentences.p2-2-s70: There remained two or three people whom I still bowed to when we met.
sentences.p2-2-s71: Included among them was Simonov, who at school among us did not stand out at all, was even‑tempered and quiet, but in him I noticed a certain independence of character and even honesty.
sentences.p2-2-s72: I don't even think that he was really very narrow‑minded.
sentences.p2-2-s73: I once had rather bright moments with him, but they did not last long and somehow suddenly became shrouded in fog.
sentences.p2-2-s74: He, apparently, was weighed down by those memories and, it seems, kept fearing that I would fall back into my former tone.
sentences.p2-2-s75: I suspected that I was very odious to him, but nevertheless I went to him, not really sure of that, probably.
sentences.p2-2-s76: One day, on a Thursday, unable to endure my solitude and knowing that on Thursdays at Anton Antonovich's the door is locked, I remembered Simonov.
sentences.p2-2-s77: Climbing up to his fourth floor, I was precisely thinking that this gentleman felt burdened by me and that I was going there in vain.
sentences.p2-2-s78: But since it always ended up in such a way — that similar considerations, as if on purpose, urged me all the more to get myself into an ambiguous situation — I went in.
sentences.p2-2-s27: But how much love, Lord, how much love I used to feel in those dreams of mine, in those "redemptions of all that is beautiful and lofty": even if a fantastical love, even if in fact never applied to anything human, yet there was so much of that love that afterwards, in practice, one didn't even feel the need to apply it: it would have been an excessive luxury.
sentences.p2-2-s28: Everything, however, always ended most felicitously in a lazy and intoxicating transition to art, that is, to the beautiful forms of existence, entirely ready, strongly stolen from poets and novelists and adapted to all kinds of services and demands.
sentences.p2-2-s29: I, for example, triumph over everyone; everyone, of course, is in the dust and compelled to voluntarily acknowledge all my perfections, and I forgive them all.
sentences.p2-2-s30: I fall in love, being a famous poet and chamberlain; I receive countless millions and at once donate them to the human race, and immediately confess before the whole people my shames, which, of course, are not mere shames but contain in themselves an extraordinary amount of the 'beautiful and lofty,' something Manfredian.
